brixs
  • brixs.Image
brixs
  • brixs.Image
  • View page source

brixs.Image

class brixs.Image(*args, **kwargs)

Returns a Image object.

Parameters:

  • data (2D array, optional) – Image.

  • filepath (string or path object, optional) – filepath.

How to initialize a Image object:

Empty

>>> im = br.Image()

From 2D array

>>> im = br.Image(data)
>>> im = br.Image(data=data)

From file

>>> im = br.Image(<filepath>)
>>> im = br.Image(filepath=<filepath>)

where filepath must point to a 2D xy-type file, where comments must be marked with # and columns must be separated by , (comma).

data

This is where we store the Image.

Type:

2D np.array

shape

Shape of data (vertical size, horizontal size), i.e., number of pixels rows and number of columns, respectively.

Type:

tuple, read only

x_centers, y_centers

pixel center labels.

Type:

np.array

Computed (read-only) attributes:
histogram (brixs.Spectrum)

Data intensity histogram.

calculated_roll (list)

Calculated rolls.

calculated_shift (list)

same as calculated_shift, but in terms of x or y centers.

columns, rows (brixs.Spectra)

Spectra obtained from each pixel columns or rows, respectively.

Write-only attributes:

None

__init__(*args, **kwargs)

Initialize the object instance.

Raises:

AttributeError – if kwargs and args cannot be read.

Methods

__init__(*args, **kwargs)

Initialize the object instance.

binning(*args, **kwargs)

Compute the 2D histogram of the data (binning of the data).

calculate_histogram([nbins])

Compute the histogram of data.

calculate_roll([axis, limit_size])

Calculate intensity misalignments via cross-correlation.

calculate_spectrum([axis])

Integrate data in one direction (sum columns or rows).

copy(*args, **kwargs)

Return a copy of the object.

crop([x_start, x_stop, y_start, y_stop])

Crop Image.

fix_curvature([deg, axis])

Roll array of pixels along a given axis to fix curvature via cc.

floor([x, y, n, nx, ny])

Set background intensity to zero.

imshow([ax, colorbar, verbose])

Display data as an image.

load(filepath[, only_data, verbose])

Load data from a text file.

pcolormesh([ax, colorbar])

Display data as a mesh.

plot(*args, **kwargs)

Same as Image.imshow.

possible_nbins()

return possible values for nbins in the y (nrows) and x (ncols) directions.

save([filepath, only_data, check_overwrite, ...])

Save data to a text file.

set_roll([value, p, f, axis])

Roll array of pixels along a given axis.

calculated_roll

calculated_shift
